preamble: The elision of the affricate [tʃ] is a rare process, as affricates typically undergo deaffrication (weakening to a fricative like [ʃ]) rather than disappearing entirely. However, total elision can occur in extreme cases of intervocalic lenition or during the simplification of complex consonant clusters.

examples.note: Syncope of the unstressed medial vowel created a cluster *facre, which then simplified entirely: the medial consonant was lost. The [tʃ] (Late Latin reflex of [k] before front vowel) did not survive.
